title: The Woman Who Made Me Retire
content: Carolyn—my wife—she looked at me one day and said, 'Peter, you have three daughters who need a father, not a legend.' That's when I knew. I was working 80-hour weeks, missing everything. The girls were growing up and I was reading annual reports. I retired at 46. Best investment I ever made.
extended_content: We'd been married almost twenty years at that point. She'd raised our daughters while I was visiting 500 companies a year. Mary's first steps? I heard about them. Annie's school play? Carolyn videotaped it. Beth's science fair? I made it—barely—and took a call in the hallway. Carolyn never complained. That made it worse. The girls understood that Dad worked. They were proud of what I did. But understanding isn't the same as having. One day Carolyn just said it plainly: 'You can always make more money. You can't make more time.' She was right. She's usually right. I walked away from Magellan the next year. Everyone thought I was crazy. Best decision I ever made.
when_to_tell: when someone is struggling with work-life balance or priorities
lesson: Some investments don't show up in financial statements. Family is the most important portfolio.
